title: Customers With Consecutive Orders
description: Find customers who have placed orders on consecutive days. Return the distinct customer names who have at least one such occurrence.
sql_solution: WITH ordered AS (SELECT customer_id, date, LAG(date) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY date) prev_date FROM orders) SELECT DISTINCT c.name FROM ordered o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id WHERE DATE(o.date) = DATE(prev_date, '+1 day');
pyspark_solution:
from pyspark.sql.functions import *
from pyspark.sql.window import Window

order_window = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(to_date(col("date")))

ordered_orders = orders.withColumn("prev_date", lag("date").over(order_window))

result = (
    ordered_orders.alias("o")
    .join(customers.alias("c"), col("o.customer_id") == col("c.id"), "inner")
    .filter(datediff(to_date(col("o.date")), to_date(col("o.prev_date"))) == 1)
    .select(col("c.name").alias("name"))
    .distinct()
)
